summary: Backend testing for the 3-tier categorization feature (Estilo de juego / Temática / Interacción). 11/11 pytest tests pass against public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Validated: (a) /api/autocomplete returns new fields subestilos+interacciones with all 13/8 default values merged + DB custom values, no case-insensitive duplicates; (b) POST/GET/PUT /api/games round-trip subestilo+interaccion; (c) /api/games/search filters by subestilo and interaccion case-insensitive partial, and combines with categoria + jugadores; (d) /api/recommend applies subestilo+interaccion as AND filter at the Mongo query level and adds them to the score, with Spanish reasons '🎨 Temática:' and '🤝 Interacción:' present. All TEST_ seed data cleaned up after run (0 leftovers verified via search).

minor.issue: categorias/subestilos/interacciones are sorted case-insensitively via `sorted(..., key=lambda s: s.lower())` which is correct, but the merge preserves the FIRST-seen casing (DB value if present, else default). If a user inserts e.g. 'eurogame' first, the default 'Eurogame' is suppressed and the lowercase variant is what UI sees — could look inconsistent vs other defaults. Consider normalising display casing for known defaults.

Player-range filter has a subtle bug: when only `jugadores_minimo` is provided (without `jugadores_maximo`), the query maps `jugadores_minimo (filter)` → `jugadores_maximo (DB) >= filter`, which is the right interpretation for 'we are N people, find games that fit'. However the field naming in SearchFilters is misleading (it's the requested player count, not the DB min/max). Not a regression introduced by this iteration but worth a rename in a future cleanup.

action_items: Optional: normalise default-value casing in /api/autocomplete so the canonical default casing wins over user-entered lowercase variants for known defaults.
Optional follow-up from iteration_2 still pending: thumbnail enrichment for /api/bgg/expansions/{id} (unrelated to this feature).

Optional: PUT /api/games/{id} cannot clear scalar fields (subestilo='' is accepted, but None is dropped). Not blocking — empty string already works as a clear since the comprehension keeps non-None values.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py L172-184 _merge_sorted is clean and correctly de-dupes case-insensitively; good helper.

server.py L685-688 _score_game returns final=50 if max_score==0 (no scoring criteria provided). That hides 'no info' as a mid-range score — could mislead the UI ordering. Consider returning a sentinel like 0 or None and letting the UI label it 'sin criterio'.

server.py L692-705 /api/recommend uses AND-filter at the Mongo query level for subestilo/interaccion. This means a recommendation request with subestilo='Espacio' will EXCLUDE games that don't have subestilo set — even if the user only meant 'preferably espacio'. Per the spec this is intentional ('los aplica como filtro AND'), but UX-wise it makes the recommender stricter than the score weights suggest (weight only 8/100). Consider exposing this as a UI hint ('Filtro estricto').
server.py L716 sort uses tuple (score, image_present) which is correct, but games without complejidad/duracion get penalised because max_score grows while score doesn't. Acceptable trade-off.
seed_data_creation: Tests create 8 TEST_-prefixed games (TEST_Autocomplete_Merge, TEST_Custom_*, TEST_Crud_Persist, TEST_Update_NewFields, TEST_Search_Eurogame_Espacio_Coop, TEST_Search_Eurogame_Granja_Compet, TEST_Search_Party_Espacio_Compet) via the module-scoped created_ids fixture; cleanup runs at teardown. Verified 0 TEST_ leftovers via /api/games/search post-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The 3-tier categorization backend (subestilo + interaccion fields on Game/GameCreate/GameUpdate/SearchFilters/RecommendRequest, autocomplete defaults+merge, search regex filter, recommend AND-filter + scoring with Spanish reasons) is fully verified end-to-end on the public endpoint. Frontend (App.js AddGame/EditGame/Search/Recommend UI with datalist autocomplete) was NOT tested per backend-only directive — Playwright targets when needed: (1) AddGameScreen ~L763-815 datalist inputs for the 3 fields; (2) EditGameScreen ~L1109 prefill; (3) SearchScreen ~L1907-1965 filters submission; (4) Recommend modal ~L295-345 with new dropdowns + Spanish reason badges in results.
